content: Just as intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) reward curious reinforcement learning agents for exploring uncertain, high-prediction-error states, an agent's own long-term memory can be framed as an intrinsic curiosity reward signal that prioritizes encoding the agent's own unvisited or uncertain memory states over mundane, predictable existing memories. This creates a self-sustaining active memory refinement loop that does not depend on external task labels.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
